place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Nostalgia a plenty in this well preserved library that houses many childhood memories for some.

Located in the quiet side of Queenstown facing sky residences @ Dawson, short walk from Queenstown MRT, a cozy library to spend your time here.

2 levels served by a lift & stairs, lots of reading areas and I particularly enjoy the level 2 quiet reading room facing the greenery.

There are lots of nostalgia and nice touches to this library.

Take your time to explore, read, borrow or exchange. what a privilege!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
As of April 2026: It is a old school library with the kinda of nostalgia you get when you visited a library in the 2000's.

Most of the Library's floor space is dedicated to books with working space being rather limited. It's not very convenient to access either as you gotta walk abit from the MRT station and not much amenities nearby.

The tables tend to get booked out until like 7pm ish. With patrons playing musical chairs where someone who has a booking asks those who didn't make a booking to move.

Interesting mix of patrons, with elderly men being the majority and somehow quite a few expats.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Hidden away in the Rifle Range greenery, Temasek Club honestly feels like one of those underrated chill spots in Singapore. The whole place has a peaceful resort vibe with lots of nature surrounding it, making it great for family gatherings, weddings, casual dinners or even just escaping the city for awhile.

The atmosphere is probably the biggest selling point here. Many visitors mention the relaxing ambience, lush views and spacious environment that makes it feel less hectic than most clubs in Singapore. There is also a decent Cantonese restaurant within with very nice menu options.

One thing to note is accessibility. If you are driving, parking is manageable and convenient. But if you rely on public transport, getting there can feel slightly out of the way since the club sits deeper into Rifle Range Road.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: On 6 March 2026, I had the privilege of visiting Temasek Club during a Taiwan–Singapore exchange meeting. The venue is beautiful and well maintained, creating a welcoming and refined atmosphere. The service was professional and attentive, and the food was excellent. It was a very pleasant and memorable experience.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
​If you're looking for the most scenic library in Singapore, this is it. Located on the 3rd floor of VivoCity, library@harbourfront is a masterclass in modern, functional design.
​Why it’s a must-visit for me.  The floor-to-ceiling windows offer a breathtaking, panoramic view of the Sentosa waterfront and the cable cars. Snagging one of the yellow deck chairs facing the water is a total game-changer.
​
Despite being in one of Singapore’s busiest malls, it is remarkably peaceful and quiet. The "coastal theme" with its undulating ceilings and blue-green hues makes the entire space feel like a sanctuary.
​
I love the clear distinction between the Adults/Teens zone and the Children's area. It keeps the reading lounges tranquil while the kids have their own interactive "Tinker Truck" and AR books to explore.

It's modern tech, highly efficient with digital newspaper stations, "Learning Pods" for TED Talks, and an easy Grab-n-Go system.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Visited Library @ HarbourFront — one of the nicest local libraries to relax and enjoy some quiet time with a book.
The view overlooking Sentosa Island adds to the calming ambiance, making it a great spot to unwind.

It’s mostly a self-service library with everything fully automated, from borrowing to returning books.
The setup is modern and efficient, making the whole experience smooth and convenient.
A great place for both readers and those looking for a peaceful retreat.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Really love the library in the middle of a crowded mall. Very happy they have a security gantry to allow people to scan passes to enter. Divided session of young kids and other section is although a hassle but necessary because the children session can be a little noisy. Also love they have the self check out gantry which is cool.

Books selection may not have the widest variety but good enough for town. I find limited seatings among bookshelves area although proper table/seats are scattered all around. I find a lot of people occupied those seats for working, studying or leisure reading. I really don't like the people taking advantage to nap there but the librarian did go around stopping them. The other point that was not pleasant are the seats facing the waterfront. While it is definitely such a good experience reading and facing sentosa, the air was really stuffy and not ventilated. The heat from the glass was intense and the air con wasn't strong enough.

Beautifully furnished library.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Jurong West Swimming Complex — Hidden Gem Alert

Best Sunday experience, hands down. Not crazy crowded, which is a rare win in Singapore. The lap pool is bliss—can actually swim freely without dodging elbows. Bonus: it’s shaded from the sun, so no need to fry while doing laps.

Even under CAT1 lightning alerts, you can still swim (huge plus). The kids’ pool is a comfortable 0.9m depth, perfect for little ones. And yes—warm showers in the children’s facilities. Game changer.

There’s a decent water park setup for kids and a surprisingly long water slide that’s way more fun than expected. Honestly, this place is the most underrated swimming complex in Singapore. Total hidden gem. Don’t sleep on it.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
The Jurong West ActiveSG was very Beautiful specially when it is Night time... It is very helpful for people of all ages specially the Older & Kids to do various kinds of Workout & Physical Activities...

As a visitor I got the Chance to visit it both Night & in the Morning... the Experience was very good

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
A multi-story complex consisting of a stadium, swimming pool with water feature like slides and lazy river. The. Complex also houses Gymnasium, child care, tuition center, dance hall, cafe.....etc.
A complex cater to all age group. You can literally spent a whole day here. Car park is at complex ground level so no worries even if it rain.
As of 23 April 2022, the children play pool is still closed due to some maintenance works

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Visited the National Library Building mainly for The Albatross File: Singapore’s Independence Declassified exhibition, and it turned out to be a meaningful experience. The exhibition itself is relatively small, but the historical significance is immense. It provides a fascinating and candid look into the events leading up to Singapore’s separation from Malaysia in 1965, including documents and notes showing that both sides had, by then, come to see separation as the likely path forward. Seeing the actual letters, transcripts, and handwritten notes made this pivotal chapter of Singapore’s history feel very real and personal

I also took some time to roam around the Lee Kong Chian Reference Library and was impressed by how incredibly well managed the entire place is. The building itself is architecturally striking, modern, spacious, and filled with natural light. The library environment feels calm, organized, and conducive for learning and research, with clear signage and well-maintained facilities throughout

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text: The National Library is conveniently located with good traffic access from around the island. It’s a comfy cool place to be if your want to study, research and just reading. You may spent hours but no worries about hunger as the are many choices of food in the area.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text: The Albatross File exhibition at NLB is a fascinating showcase of Singapore’s separation history, featuring declassified documents, oral histories, and immersive multimedia displays. It brings to life the tense years leading up to independence, blending archival records with dramatized storytelling.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
2026 January - Did quick look around, enjoyed reading the stories from the mini exhibition Knots of Time. Looks like there are other exhibitions and all sorts of collection, will be back to explore and read.

Great ambience, nice for researchers and the public alike. Place has plenty of space if you'd like to get some work done, offers brilliant views, and even has a skygarden. Power and water is readily available, and theres even sufficient space for a stroll if you're feeling a mental block coming on.

Will be back to check out the albatross files. The atrium at the bottom is very pleasant, and the vaguely 'heritage' old chang kee seems like a good place to check out as well.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Albatross exhibition of Singapore

Thankful for this curated history that bares all that Singaporeans may not know. We had the privilege of having seniors in their 70s who came along, cementing our understanding of what they had gone thru.

The whole setup allowed us to go at our own pace and the voice of our late and retired ministers add a sense of urgency and realism.

A suggestion for those who are not English educated, as the ministers are speaking in English, please allow translator set made avail in various language to our non English speaking seniors.

Lastly the wooden board is super heavy. Made it very difficult for us to lift up the board to read the files.  Lastly is there a need to have the board hidden? Most don’t know it exist until someone lifted it up.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: I have headed here in singapore to head to IMM mall. Metro ride was good. Im thrilled to see everyone using metro besides personal transport. Most of the people are very friendly giving directions when needed. Security team and staff are so helpful in showing the directions. I depended so much on them as I visited and took train for the first time. There was some confusion when taking the circle change in the station.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
I love in the West. I've been using the JE MRst exchange since 1991. The ceiling plan hasn't changed at all since then.

Until recently, this roof wasn't washed much at all and even now it's hard to get rid ofnth grime and bird waste accumulating here. I understand that this is very difficult given the business of the interchange. But something has to be done.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: This MRT Station is strategically located in Jurong East with two popular shopping malls in the area directly connected to it and with the IMM shopping mall and the Ng Teng Fong Community Hospital just a bit farther away from the aforementioned two malls. Therefore this MRT Station sees a heavy footfall. As I live in Hillview, I can easily get to Jurong East by taking bus number 184 followed by a one stop MRT ride from Clementi MRT Station to Jurong East MRT Station.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
I cannot believe my eyes! Totally different from 20 years ago.

I have not been to Jurong East MRT station for 20 years. When I stepped out of the train nowadays, I was shocked. It has changed so much!

20 years ago, this was just a normal station. Now, it looks like a whole new modern city. It is very interesting and well-developed. Here is why I was so impressed:

If you have not been here in a long time, you must visit. It is amazing to see how much Singapore has built and improved this area. It is a true transformation!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Jurong East MRT Station is a very convenient interchange station connecting both the North-South Line and East-West Line. It is well organized and makes travelling across Singapore easier, especially for people working or living in the west area.

The station is directly connected to nearby shopping malls like JEM and Westgate, which is very useful for shopping and dining after travel. Clean environment and clear signboards make transfers smooth even during busy hours. Highly recommended interchange station in Singapore.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: ​The park's main activities are conveniently clustered around McDonald's, which serves as a great central spot for dining, socializing, and recreation. Aside from active play, it is a fantastic place for gatherings, featuring camping grounds, barbecue pits, large open lawns for picnics, kite flying and jogging.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
West Coast Park is a long but relatively quiet park that stretches along Singapore’s western coastline. It’s a wonderful place for a relaxed outdoor day, especially for families with kids

One of the highlights is the large playground, which is one of the better ones in Singapore. The climbing structures, slides, and play areas keep children entertained for a long time, and there’s plenty of open space nearby for running around

The park is also great for cycling and casual walks. You can easily rent bicycles or family pedal carts near the entrance and ride along the park connectors while enjoying the sea breeze

For those who enjoy spending more time outdoors, the park has BBQ pits and camping areas, making it perfect for weekend gatherings with friends and family. There are also scenic spots along the waterfront where people fish or simply relax and watch the ships in the distance

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I do like to come to this nature park by the sea - to take in the salty air and the cool sea breeze, both of which relaxes and then invigorates me. I like to hear the waves crashing against the shore and to be among the greenery in this nature park - in particular, the bamboo fringed walkway as regards the latter. The amenities at this nature park is adequate - with enough car parking spaces and public toilets. And overall, this nature park is well-maintained.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Well maintained park and a good place to spend time alone or with family and friends. Suitable for all ages, whether to exercise in the mornings or catch a lovely sunset in the evenings.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
West Coast Park is considerably smaller than East Coast Park (ECP), but it remains a highly viable zone for R&R. I have scouted the perimeter and identified several optimal coordinates with good sightlines to hold position and decompress.

Civilian population density in this grid is minimal. The decreased foot traffic results in a secure perimeter and a highly tranquil atmosphere—ideal for lowering your guard and recovering stamina.

Be advised on two minor variables:

First, visuals on the maritime border are compromised and do not match the aesthetics of ECP.

Second, heavy transport vessels are active in the adjacent waters. My MJOLNIR armor’s environmental sensors occasionally detected industrial exhaust drifting into the area. It is non-lethal, but noticeable without a helmet filtration system.

Overall assessment: A solid, quiet sector for downtime. Proceed to coordinates at your discretion.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This shopping mall is highly convenient and offers an impressive variety of stores, making it a place where you can find almost anything you need. The directory includes a well-stocked supermarket, numerous fashion boutiques, electronics retailers, and a wide selection of dining options ranging from quick bites to sit-down restaurants. The layout is generally logical, though it can take some time to navigate due to the sheer size of the complex. It serves as an excellent one-stop destination for both daily necessities and specialty items. Facilities like restrooms and parking are well-maintained and accessible. Given the extensive range of shops and services available under one roof, it is a highly practical choice for weekend errands or casual browsing. However, because of its comprehensive nature, it tends to attract large crowds, especially during peak hours and weekends. Overall, it provides a highly efficient and reliable shopping experience for visitors looking to complete multiple tasks in a single trip.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: A nice, bustling shopping mall set over multiple levels. Lots of restaurants and takeaways across multiple levels, plus a large Kopitiam food court on Level 6. Besides the eateries and 'regular shops' there is a cinema, games arcades and various niche stores, plus access to the Dhoby Ghaut MRT station. Notably clean restrooms.

review_text: I visited here for a morning walk. The park is quite large, so you'll definitely work up a sweat walking around. I highly recommend bringing a water bottle. It's full of nature, and spotting a wild chicken was a fun experience.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

review_text: This is a quiet green park in Singapore. Looks like an old park with lot of greenery. It's clam, quiet and with lot of shade its ideal for a relaxing time. The famous Fort canning tree tunnel is also located here. Entrance is free. Popular place amongst the tourists. Good place to relax.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
A beautiful, relaxing and vibrant place for walks, for taking a step back from the hectic pace.
Spending 3 hours walking and appreciating the fresh air, the manicured gardens, the lake and wonderful architecture, helped to destress and reset.
If you've not been, well worth some time out.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Definitely one of the nicest and must go to parks in Singapore if you are alone, couples, families group of friends wanting to escape from the concrete jungle of the city. Jurong used to be perceived as a very faraway venue to commute to but with so many new MRT train lines it’s so much easier and faster to reach. Do take note of opening hours. Free entry and easily accessible with many entrances by Chinese Garden MRT or Lakeside MRT and buses. Great for all activities, walking , strolling, jogging, cycling, picnics and there is even a skate park and dog run. What makes this park stand out from the rest is the sheer size, the large lake with canoeing; stunning replicas of historical Chinese and Japanese architecture and pagodas, bonsai garden and wildlife. And especially the stunning Lily Flower Pond at the Japanese Garden and the Lotus Flower Pond at the Chinese Garden. Definitely worth to make a trip down to enjoy this green oasis escapade rich in flora and fauna and water. It’s very tranquil and peaceful from Mondays to Saturdays. Ample toilets and drinking water dispenser and also a beverage shop selling nice cold beverages.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
NParks really did a good job in revamping this park.
There's plenty of interesting plants and wildlife to spot.
Took me and my friends 3 hours to casually explore the entire area.
It's definitely a nice place to run in the mornings/evenings.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I visit Jurong Lake Gardens quite frequently since I am staying nearby. The walking tracks are well-maintained, and the view around the lake at anytime is just impressive. It’s a wonderful place to clear your mind and enjoy the beautiful scenery during the walks.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
I highly recommend to visit this gardens, as there are a lot of attractions to see and they are all free! It’s a beautiful area to dive into culture and nature, just 40 min from Singapore with the bus. Very relaxing and a moment to take a pause from the overtourism and consumistic view of marina bay and the Singapore center.
Not as big as the botanic garden, but I’d say it’s better to view this than that.

Not enough shaded canopies for users of the facility to keep their belongings the heat on hot sunny days. No food vending machines for snacks, only hv a drinks vending machine.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Fun time with kiddos. The toilets are great with large cubicles and compartments to hang your dry clothes. Definitely a comeback for my family.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Although I must say that I missed the huge slides that my generation grew up with. But it is a nice transitional change to me being a Dad now, bringing my little kiddos in what is now a kids play area.

Also the toilets have had a face-lift. Kids changing room is nice and clean. Adults changing room is equally good.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: They had a baby pool, kids playground, kids splash area, lazy river and a wave pool. Wave pool had waves every hour lasting about 15mins each time. Lazy river is where we spent most of our time at, easy going and fun. Rates are affordable with activesg. Minus one star because I'd prefer having the slides.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Went with family on a Saturday. Place was busy but still have space for a good swim. Kids enjoyed the new water play area and wave pool (though wave was mild and very far apart). The lazy river is still there.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
We visited IMM Building, Singapore’s largest outlet mall, and had a good shopping experience. This place really lives up to what many reviewers say — it has all varieties from high-end brands to low-end shops, making it easier to find what you need under one roof. You can discover everything from fashion and sportswear to electronics, home goods, and plenty of dining options.

Many visitors also love the discounts on big brands (up to around 80% at outlet stores), and the mall is spacious and easy to navigate. There are lots of food and beverage outlets to take a break while shopping.

Crowds can get heavier on weekends, and some items in outlet stores may not be the latest season stock, but overall it’s a great place for bargain hunting and casual shopping.

Overall experience was pleasant — 4 stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐.

Hum log IMM Building gaye aur overall shopping experience kaafi achhi rahi. Jaise online reviews mein bhi log likhte hain, yahan high-end se leke low-end tak sab varieties mil jaati hain, jis se shopping easy ho jati hai — fashion, sportswear, electronics, home goods aur bahut saare food options sab ek hi jagah par.

Bohot saare visitors ko big brands par ache discounts bhi milte hain aur mall ka layout kaafi spacious hai jisme easily explore kar sakte ho.

Weekend par thoda crowd zyada ho sakta hai aur outlet stores mein sab items latest season ke nahi milte, lekin overall yeh mall bargain hunting aur casual shopping ke liye ek achhi jagah hai.

Overall experience achha raha — 4 stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Singapore's only outlet mall with all major brands under one roof. You will get good deals from top brands here. Don't forget to plan to visit here when you travel to Singapore. Must visit mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
IMM Mall is a great spot for shopping, especially if you’re looking for outlet deals. It has a wide range of stores, including brands like Coach, Michael Kors, Kate Spade, Charles & Keith, Pedro, Cotton On, and plenty of footwear outlets.
Getting there is easy—take the MRT to Jurong East MRT Station and follow the signs. It’s about a 10 minute walk, and you’ll pass JEM on the way.
There are also lots of dining options, making it a good place to spend a few hours. If you’re shopping, if you spend over SGD 100 in a store, you can request a GST refund receipt—just show your passport at the time of purchase. You can then claim the refund at the airport during departure.
Overall, a solid choice if you’re planning a shopping trip in Singapore.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
For my family and me living in the west, IMM is one of our our go-to spots for shopping, leisure, and meals because it is just so convenient.
Also, being directly connected to Jurong East MRT and the Bus Interchange makes it exceptionally accessible.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Have visited IMM since I was a kid. That was like decades ago. Since then, new wings were constructed, parking has expanded, new links to the Jurong shopping cluster has been built and stores have come and go. Really like how there everything here, from daily necessities to outlet stores. Weekend parking is a nightmare though and the gantry should be upgraded to reduce the jam at the carpark entrance. Also, glad that level 3 is pet friendly thanks to the new BYD cafe.

Jaise online reviews mein bhi log likhte hain, yahan high-end se leke low-end tak sab varieties mil jaati hain, jis se shopping easy ho jati hai — fashion, sportswear, electronics, home goods aur bahut saare food options sab ek hi jagah par.

Bohot saare visitors ko big brands par ache discounts bhi milte hain aur mall ka layout kaafi spacious hai jisme easily explore kar sakte ho.

Weekend par thoda crowd zyada ho sakta hai aur outlet stores mein sab items latest season ke nahi milte, lekin overall yeh mall bargain hunting aur casual shopping ke liye ek achhi jagah hai.

Overall experience achha raha — 4 stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Singapore's only outlet mall with all major brands under one roof. You will get good deals from top brands here. Don't forget to plan to visit here when you travel to Singapore. Must visit mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
IMM Mall is a great spot for shopping, especially if you’re looking for outlet deals. It has a wide range of stores, including brands like Coach, Michael Kors, Kate Spade, Charles & Keith, Pedro, Cotton On, and plenty of footwear outlets.
Getting there is easy—take the MRT to Jurong East MRT Station and follow the signs. It’s about a 10 minute walk, and you’ll pass JEM on the way.
There are also lots of dining options, making it a good place to spend a few hours. If you’re shopping, if you spend over SGD 100 in a store, you can request a GST refund receipt—just show your passport at the time of purchase. You can then claim the refund at the airport during departure.
Overall, a solid choice if you’re planning a shopping trip in Singapore.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
For my family and me living in the west, IMM is one of our our go-to spots for shopping, leisure, and meals because it is just so convenient.
Also, being directly connected to Jurong East MRT and the Bus Interchange makes it exceptionally accessible.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Have visited IMM since I was a kid. That was like decades ago. Since then, new wings were constructed, parking has expanded, new links to the Jurong shopping cluster has been built and stores have come and go. Really like how there everything here, from daily necessities to outlet stores. Weekend parking is a nightmare though and the gantry should be upgraded to reduce the jam at the carpark entrance. Also, glad that level 3 is pet friendly thanks to the new BYD cafe.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Jurong Point has really evolved into one of the largest and most comprehensive suburban malls in Singapore over the years. The mall keeps expanding and now offers an impressive variety of restaurants, cafés, retail shops, supermarkets, and leisure options all under one roof. There are also at least two sizeable food courts with plenty of local and international food choices, making it easy to find something for everyone

One of the biggest advantages is the excellent convenience - it has always been directly connected to both the MRT station and the bus interchange, making it extremely accessible rain or shine. Despite being a heartland mall, it feels vibrant and lively with many different sections to explore, from Japanese-themed food streets to family-friendly spaces and lifestyle shops

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Jurong Point is one of the more convenient suburban malls in Singapore with a huge variety of shops, supermarkets, restaurants and daily necessities all under one roof. The mall is directly connected to Boon Lay MRT, so accessibility is definitely a big plus.

What I like most is the wide food selection ranging from local hawker-style eateries to proper restaurants and cafes. It’s also spacious enough for long walks without feeling too cramped on weekdays.

However, the mall can get extremely crowded during weekends and peak dining hours, especially around the food areas. Some sections also feel slightly dated compared to newer malls in Singapore, and navigating between the different wings can be a bit confusing initially.

Overall, still a very practical and convenient mall for shopping, dining and everyday errands in the west side of Singapore.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Didn’t expect tiramisu to hit this good, but here we are at Tirato Tiramisu location at B1 near boast.

Caved and tried two flavours — pistachio and yam — and honestly? No regrets. Just me wondering why I didn’t get more

Pistachio was rich, nutty, and somehow still light? Super creamy, but not the kind that makes you feel jelak. The subtle crunch inside? Chef’s kiss. Definitely feels a little luxe.

Yam caught me off guard — it’s soft, naturally sweet, and has that nostalgic vibe, like something you grew up eating but upgraded. Super comforting and lowkey addictive.

What made it even better:
✨ No pork, no lard, no alcohol — totally kid-friendly (my little cousin loved it)
✨ Freshly made daily — no preservatives, no colouring, nothing artificial
✨ So many flavours to choose from — I was stuck at the counter for 5 mins trying to pick. Spoilt for choice fr.

They’re only $6.80 each, which feels like a steal for the quality.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Very fun shopping at Jurong point. Lots of things to see for Chinese new year from ornaments to cookies in jars and gift boxes. Got god of fortune everywhere too. Pineapple tarts and more ~

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Jurong point is huge! Discover their Japanese street, Mongkok street for restaurants, bakeries and bubble teas. Donki, Scarlett, Thai Supermarket for international selection of snacks and products. One whole day shopping, lunch, tea breaks and dinner to explore about. Definitely a must visit in SG

review_text:
You can always find some hidden gems there! Basically, if we see a stall with a long queue… we just try it 😂

🍗 Hainanese Chicken Rice — We love chicken rice! The chicken was okay, some parts were a bit dry, but still satisfying as our first chicken rice in Singapore this year.

🍜 Mixed Hor Fun — Loved it with a mix of meats and seafood! Not a big fan of the burnt parts, but overall still enjoyable.

🥤 Sugar Cane Juice — So refreshing, especially in the hot weat

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Visit there on a Saturday morning about 9am. Most of the stalls are not operating yet. However we found 2 stalls that gives real value for money. The wanton mee stall ( facing the blocks behind) served a sumptuous bowl of noodles with 4 wantons and a generous helping of char siew at $4. Have eaten at other hawker centres I have not seen such portions. The prawn noodles is even better. Their normal sized prawns were so big it's like a big prawns and it's only $4.50. Worth another view.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
0 star for the char kway tiao and hokkien mee(#01-13)! They are terrible! No fragrance just full of salty burnt taste. Yucks.

The dumplings were decent.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Stopped by Ding Sheng, unit #01-78. Great service by this 'uncle'. Ordered Mui Fan, $5.50. Average, nothing exceptional. Tried Aunty Jo's dessert, ice jelly with sea coconut, $2.80. This place gets a little crowded from 7pm onwards. Also try Hane Chiang Thai food , #01-21. Tom Yum soup is ok.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: The Thunder Tea Story. Came to AV food centre for lunch and am glad to find a healthy food option. Affordable at $5.50 (option for white or brown rice) and extra charge for any add-on option . I added Chai Por Neng (Egg with Radish) for some protein at extra $1.50.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: One-North is a very well-structured MRT station, perfectly servicing one of Singapore's major business and industrial districts. The layout is refreshingly straightforward, making it a station where you're unlikely to ever be late due to navigation issues. With multiple exits that integrate directly into the surrounding buildings, it’s a 'get the job done' kind of stop that feels custom-built for the busy professionals working in the area.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: You would love the nice aroma when you come out of the train in the morning. Still can't figure out from which store. A lot of good food options. Cold storage at the MRT level.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
🟧One-north MRT Station
🟨 9 Ayer Rajah Ave, Singapore 138647

🟦One-North Station is connected to the integrated office/retail complexes of Fusionopolis, Connexis, Symbiosis and Biopolis and other office towers.

🟧 The Insead Business School is in close proximity to the One-North station.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: New, clean, convenient access MRT station which connects to the circle line (yellow line). There are more and more restaurants, bars  opened nearby, you could also find stationary shop and clinic.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: I think it is quite a remote area in the west. However, the food and other amenities are fairly good, and it is a relatively concentrated office district.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: A bustling neighborhood mall! This mall is very convenient since it's directly linked to the Clementi MRT and bus interchange. It’s got everything you need across several floors, from a variety of shops and banks to a supermarket, food court, and even a library. It’s the perfect all-in-one spot for locals to grab a meal or run errands.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
The Clementi Mall is a very convenient small-to-medium sized heartland mall, especially because it is directly connected to Clementi MRT station and the bus interchange. This makes it one of the easiest malls to access in the west, and it naturally gets very crowded on weekends and during peak hours

The tenant mix is practical and useful, with food options, shops, services, a supermarket, pharmacies, and casual dining places. It is not a huge destination mall, but it works very well for daily needs, quick meals, errands, and meeting up with family or friends

The lower floors can feel quite packed, especially near the MRT entrance and popular food areas. The upper floors are noticeably less crowded, so it is more comfortable if you want to walk around or browse at a slower pace

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
The Clementi Mall: A very convenient and modern shopping hub.

I visited around 7 PM on a weekday. The mall is directly connected to the MRT station, making it extremely convenient for a quick stop or a dinner visit.

The Shops & Atmosphere:
There is a great variety of shops and restaurants, including a section with many food stalls that feels like a mini-market. I was very surprised to find a massive library right inside the mall! It’s a great place to browse and borrow books in a modern setting.

The Food & Culture:
One "minus point" for me is that the mall mostly contains international brands and chain stores. I didn't find many small, local shops, so it might not be the best place if you are looking to explore traditional Singaporean culture.

The Verdict:
It is a perfect, air-conditioned spot for practical shopping and dining, but it feels more like a standard modern mall than a cultural destination.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This mall is small but very high-traffic because of the students and the MRT connection. It’s functional and has all the basics like a big library and plenty of quick-service food. If you want a more authentic experience, the Clementi 448 Market nearby is where the "real" West side food is, though it’s always hot and noisy.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Clementi mall is a place my family would go almost every weekends.
A place where we enjoy most of the local food at B1.  The recent visit at the B1 food area is disappointed, the indoor tempetature made us stay away of the poor aircon ventilation.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Outstanding library. Convenient location (OPP Science Centre) and many floors or materials. Their program room is great for kid activities.

Surprisingly, toilets are badly maintained :(

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Nice, quiet and with lots of books. Highly recommended library of you love reading!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Amazing! Just no words to say. It was my first time to the Jurong regional library and the very scale of it took me in awe. So many collections of different books and so many different levels to go and explore! They have a community space for events and workshops , then there is a cafe and bakery inside with good food options. There is so many different things to explore! I couldn’t even finish going through all the sections! I will definitely go there again soon. The place is also located very close to the MRT  station and the Jurong east bus terminus . It has a sheltered walkway so we could easily reach the place even though it was raining. Many people from all different age groups were there. There is study tables with charging points. For some of the study tables you need to book in advance but for the rest you can always find and empty space. There are DVDs and other stuff as well. If you have to borrow books you need to register , but I just went for reading so I didn’t have to register anything. The library closes at 9pm and is open all days of the week. They will announce over the mike before the closing time so you have time to pack your stuff. I especially liked the photography section and farming section . There’s a separate magazine section and newspaper section as well. Different languages from Tamil to Malay to Korean you will find in the library .

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: This library was visited few times in the span of 15 years. Still impressed by its cleanliness, calm and  peaceful atmosphere in addition to the silent air, the display of new arrivals also catch my eyes. Always full of study-loaded students and other book-lovers, you will still find a seat for enjoying some books or surfing internet on the provided PC's as the library is very spacious. Remarkably, there is a vast collection of books.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Spacious JE Centre Library with many seats availability. Cool Air-Cond Environment, Front Welcoming Display, Information,  Photo Wall Taking, Green Wall Digital Huge Screen & Clean well maintained Toilet. Great Place for Reading, Desktop PC Computer Internet access & comfy sofas. Wonderful!👏🇸🇬

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
We visited Maxwell Food Centre at around 8:00 AM on a Monday, so it was still relatively quiet with very few queues. Unfortunately, the famous chicken rice stall was closed, which was a bit disappointing. This was actually my second visit, and I still haven't had the chance to try it!

Thankfully, there are plenty of other stalls serving chicken rice, so we simply picked one without a line and it didn't disappoint. In fact, that's the beauty of Maxwell Food Centre. There are so many food options to choose from, and everything we tried was delicious. The drinks were great too, and the prices are very affordable. Those in the pictures are only some of those we found really delicious.

Whether you're craving local favorites or just looking for a good meal, Maxwell Food Centre never disappoints. If you're in Singapore, make sure to stop by for delicious food at budget-friendly prices. Just keep in mind that if you're hoping to try the famous chicken rice, it's worth checking the stall's opening hours before you go!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There’s a wide variety of food and the laksa was absolutely amazing. It’s cheaper if you pay cash instead of card. The staffs in charge of cleaning the trays were really proactive. We didn’t try the super crowded stalls, but the ones we had were pretty solid in terms of quality. Also no bad odor

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
I was here in the evening time and most of the stalls were closed for the day.
But I am very fortunate of finding a chicken rice shop still open.
Uncle Louis Famous chicken rice. Uncle was very hands on. He takes each customer's order attentively and gives suggestions along the way.
I ordered chicken with soup and veggies. Very tender, fresh veggie sides and hot rice. Yummy! Welcome local dinner on my 1st night in Singapore.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
A step back in time with fantastic food variety at really bargain prices. Only one of the 3 stall rows has high velocity fans for extra cooling. A few obvious winners among the vendors. We had Vietnamese  Tofu and noodles and vegetarian rice paper rolls.
A local character has found a way to always get his favorite seat - Xtra load rap music. He managed to get a whole table under a fan for himself during the lunch peak. There's someone who understands marketing!
A fantastic authentic street food location that has a busy lunch peak but is much quieter otherwise.
The lady selling fresh crushed fruit and sugar can drinks is a standout nearthe China Town exit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: My favourite eating place in the city by far. So much to choose from, foodie heaven. There's always a good selection of shops open. Can be very busy at time with queues for the most popular places.  Don't be scared to share tables. When finished, put your tray in designated area before leaving.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
*Dapur 3A Power Kacang Pool @Ayer Rajah Food Centre*

It's been a while that I feel compelled to give a review but after returning for a second helping I knew I wanted to. I'm truly not only impressed by the delish Middle Eastern classics Dapur 3A Power Kacang Pool serves but that they really give deep meaning to a degustation delight to make both your happy mouth and stomach dance with joy. And mind you being of mixed heritage my palate is rather well exposed.

First off, there is no denying, like their namesake, their signature kacang pool is an absolute sublime delight and winner. The texture of the fava beans or foul medames base is beautifully textured. Not too pureed and with enough light ruffage. Knowing how those who love the soulful pleasurable dish would want either a little more or less they've got a double egg version and add-ons one can easily customise. Now their dish that bowls me over is their shabsuka. I've had and sampled plenty, and theirs is a wonderfully balanced one that everyone in our home who had a bite agree is sensational. It's sweet but from the chunky tomatoes used so it glides well. And the spices used elevates its nuanced flavour. And today with my choice of beef adding a rich flavour threading through its already lovely base it's a flavour bomb from the first taste.

Now the one thing that to me helps literally mop and absorb the delicious flavours has to be their bread. Expecting a lightly toasted French baguette or local loaf version I was not prepared but was pleasantly delighted by their softer version. I was so glad I ordered extra for it's such a clever and brilliant pairing to the dish selections they offer.

I'll make it simpler. It is hard not to grab something from here when we swing over to this rather nondescript heartland food centre, and so should you. Also, the kind smiling lady who made and served the food is just so sweet and friendly.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
A good and spacious Food Centre that offers delicious food at a reasonable price.
For vegans and vegetarians, there’s a stall serving delicious local Chinese vegan food (V4 Vegetarian) ~ made without MSG, eggs, milk and alliums. It’s 100% vegan and Pay-as-you-wish (one can decide how much  to pay). I sincerely hope more people can come and support this stall, as it’s truly inspiring to see a business put so much heart, love and compassion into their dishes.
There are also stalls that serve good Teh Halia (Ginger Milk Tea) and Teh Pandan (Pandan Tea) at truly reasonable prices.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
@Ayer Rajah Food Centre

If you’re craving authentic Indian-style Mee Siam, the Mee Siam Mama with rusuk at Ayer Rajah Food Centre is seriously one of the hidden gems in the west. The gravy is rich, spicy, tangy and full of depth — very different from the usual Malay-style mee siam. The bee hoon soaks up the flavorful kuah perfectly, while the sambal gives it an addictive kick without overpowering the dish.

What really steals the show is the rusuk (ribs). Tender, juicy and packed with flavor, it pairs amazingly well with the spicy gravy. Add the crispy tofu cubes, lime, boiled egg and fresh spring onions, and every spoonful becomes comfort food at its best.

This is proper old-school “Mee Siam Mama” done right — hearty, messy, spicy and deeply satisfying. You can tell this stall has perfected the recipe over the years. One of the few Indian stalls in Singapore still serving this unique version of Mee Siam, and definitely worth travelling for if you love authentic hawker food. Highly recommended for anyone who enjoys bold flavors and traditional Indian-Muslim hawker dishes.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: horrible kunafa sold by shawarma man stall. ordered pistachio kunafa and it was very oily, little to no cheese, and a huge load of pistachio sauce.  pay $12 to get diabetes and oil

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: This food stall is great — the food is delicious, affordable, and generous in portion. It’s run by an elderly couple, and every time I come, they always serve a lot. Sometimes, they're even worried you might not have enough to eat, so they’ll give you an extra bowl of soup. Truly a stall run by a kind-hearted couple — highly recommended!
